mnemonics.title: DAC Key Features
mnemonics.content:
DAC = Decentralized + Autonomous + Corporation

Remember: D-A-C = Digital, Automated, Code-driven organization

Or: DAC = Decentralized operations + Automated execution + Corporate governance

acronyms.usage: An organization that runs on blockchain with automated governance through smart contracts
Broader concept; DAC is a commercial subtype focused on profit generation
Software application on blockchain; a DAC typically consists of one or more DApps
Method used by DACs to raise capital by issuing governance tokens

visualTips.visualization: Draw a pyramid: Top = Smart Contracts (rules engine), Middle = Token Holders (voting), Bottom = Blockchain (immutable ledger). Show arrows from holders to contracts representing proposals and voting, and from contracts to blockchain representing execution.
Two columns: Left (Traditional) = CEO → Board → Employees → Customers. Right (DAC) = Token Holders ↔ Smart Contracts ↔ Blockchain ↔ Global Network. Show the direct disintermediation in DAC.
Show token holder → submits proposal → all token holders vote → smart contract counts votes → if threshold met → automatic execution on blockchain → result recorded immutably.

analogies.analogy: Just as a vending machine automatically dispenses products after payment without human intervention, a DAC automatically executes corporate actions after voting thresholds are met without manual approval.
Just as traditional corporations have bylaws defining how they operate, smart contracts in a DAC serve as digital, self-executing bylaws that cannot be violated.

analogies.concept: Tokenized equity as digital stock certificates
analogies.analogy: Just as stock certificates represent ownership in a company, blockchain tokens represent ownership in a DAC - but unlike paper certificates, they can be transferred instantly globally.
